title: Architecture: Metrics and Observability
bodyMarkdown:
Overview of how Helios collects and exports operational metrics.

## Collection
src/metrics/stats.ts holds an in-process buffer of samples, plus histogram buckets for latency and size distributions used across serving and restore. Any module can call record with a metric name, value, and tags; nothing about the caller needs to know how the sample gets shipped out.

## Export
src/metrics/exporter.ts drains the buffer on an interval and posts it to the collector. If the flush fails, samples in that batch are dropped rather than retried, we intentionally favor a lossy exporter over one that can build unbounded backlog during an outage.

## Dashboards
The shared dashboard covers request latency percentiles, replica health, restore duration, and shard cache hit rate. On-call should know where this lives before their first shift, see the on-call guide.

## Gaps
Restore progress is not currently exported as a metric, only start and completion are visible. A feature request is open to expose incremental progress.

## Owner
Elena Petrova owns the metrics and exporter modules and the dashboard.
